g.text: a few frames of video (the clip)
48 channels · ~1/4 the frames · 1/16 the height & width
Why it matters here: the DiT never touches pixels — it denoises this small latent (figs 1–10), which is why video diffusion is affordable. And because the VAE is CAUSAL, lingbot feeds
frames in as they arrive and grows the latent chunk-by-chunk (streaming), instead of waiting for the whole clip — essential for a real-time closed-loop world model.
latent. The VAE is CAUSAL, so the latent is built chunk-by-chunk as frames stream in (no need for the whole clip).
The pixels (3, F, H, W) are compressed to a 48-channel latent (48, F/4, H/16, W/16) — far fewer numbers — and decoded back to ≈ the same video. The DiT works entirely in this
Fig 11b · the VAE, illustrated — squeeze the clip into a tiny latent, then decode it back
